REJECTED_CANDIDATE.fires_when: Any table candidate found by pdfplumber or hrule strategy but rejected by _is_quality_table

CAPTION_NEARBY.note: v2: narrowed to Table-only; Figure/Chart/Exhibit removed

NUMERIC_COLUMN_ALIGNMENT.fires_when: Total of 3+ lines across all paragraph blocks with 3+ tokens and 2+ numeric tokens

LEADER_DOT_ROWS.fires_when: Source 1: rejected candidate with dot_leader in rejection_reasons; Source 2: 3+ paragraph lines matching leader-dot pattern across all blocks

DOC_TABLE_HEAVY.fires_when: Document is classified as table_heavy and page has no extracted tables
DOC_TABLE_HEAVY.note: Rarely fires; most docs classify as native_text or layout_heavy

family_independence_rule.base_rule: expected=true iff risk signals from >= 2 distinct families fire

exception_v3.name: parser_plus_numeric_only
exception_v3.description: parser + NUMERIC_COLUMN_ALIGNMENT without CAPTION_NEARBY, LEADER_DOT_ROWS, or DOC_TABLE_HEAVY is downgraded from true to unknown
exception_v3.rationale: Both parser and numeric_column_alignment react to the same visual structure on chart pages (data axes, callouts, annotations). They are empirically correlated, not independent. A third independent cue is required for expected=true.
exception_v3.condition: risk_families == {parser, text} AND NUMERIC_COLUMN_ALIGNMENT in risk_names AND CAPTION_NEARBY not in risk_names

parser_configuration.note: Pages with > 3000 chars skip pdfplumber entirely (no rejected candidates generated)

validation_v3.remaining_fp_driver: parser + caption_nearby on pages with incidental Table X references
